Resistant Hypertension (AHA 2018 + ACC 2017):

(1) นิยาม: BP ≥ 130/80 (or ≥ 140/90 per old) แม้ใช้ 3 ยาขนาดเหมาะสม + 1 ตัวต้องเป็น thiazide
detailed_explanation.reason: Resistant HTN: confirm true vs pseudo (adherence, white-coat); workup secondary (esp primary aldo); switch HCTZ → chlorthalidone; spironolactone 4th-line (PATHWAY-2). Lifestyle. Specialist if refractory.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 42 ปี HTN ตั้งแต่อายุ 35, ใช้ 3 ยา BP ยังไม่ controlled + K+ 3.2 + adrenal incidentaloma

choices.label: A
choices.text: Add more thiazide diuretic without workup

detailed_explanation.summary:
Primary Aldosteronism (PA) Screening + Workup (Endocrine Society 2016):

(1) Indications screen: resistant HTN, HTN + hypokalemia (spontaneous or diuretic-induced), HTN + adrenal mass, HTN onset < 30 yo, severe HTN (> 150/100), HTN + family history early stroke/HTN, HTN + 1st-degree relative with PA, HTN + OSA
detailed_explanation.reason: Primary aldosteronism — most common secondary HTN (~10% resistant). Screen with ARR (correct K first). Confirm with suppression. CT + AVS for subtype. Adenoma → surgery; bilateral → MRA. Family medicine triggers referral.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Diabetic Foot Ulcer Outpatient Management (IDSA + ADA + IWGDF)

detailed_explanation.summary:
Diabetic Foot Ulcer Outpatient Management (IDSA + ADA + IWGDF):

(1) Comprehensive assessment: depth (Wagner/UT classification), size, infection signs (PEDIS), vascular (ABI, pulses, transcutaneous O2), neuropathy (10g monofilament, vibration), osteomyelitis (probe to bone, X-ray, MRI if suspected)
detailed_explanation.reason: DFU: comprehensive assessment (depth, infection, vascular, neuropathy, OM). Empiric oral abx if mild infection. Offloading critical (total contact cast). Vascular assessment. Wound care + glycemic + smoking. Multidisciplinary foot clinic reduces amputation 50-85%.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 60 ปี ASCVD risk 18% — เริ่ม atorvastatin 40 → muscle pain ทั่วร่างกาย 3 wk; CK 280 (mild ↑); lipids LDL 142

choices.label: D

detailed_explanation.summary:
Statin-Associated Muscle Symptoms (SAMS) Approach (ACC + NLA):

(1) Assess + classify: myalgia (pain, normal CK) — most common ~5-10%
detailed_explanation.reason: SAMS: rule out alternative causes; stop statin 2-4 wk; rechallenge with alternative or lower dose (70-90% tolerate). Non-statin: ezetimibe, PCSK9i, bempedoic, inclisiran for true intolerance. SAMSON — much is nocebo. Lifestyle + holistic ASCVD risk.

choices.label: A

choices.label: E
choices.text: High-dose fish oil OTC with no monitoring

detailed_explanation.summary:
Severe Hypertriglyceridemia (HTG) Management (Endocrine Society + AHA):

(1) Risk stratification: TG 150-499 (moderate), 500-1000 (severe), > 1000 (very severe — high pancreatitis risk)
detailed_explanation.reason: Severe HTG: pancreatitis risk if > 500-1000. Treat secondary (alcohol, DM, obesity, drugs). Lifestyle foundation (alcohol cessation here critical). Fibrate first-line + icosapent ethyl (REDUCE-IT). Statin for LDL/ASCVD. Genetic forms refer.

choices.label: A
choices.text: GDMT same as HFrEF — start all 4 pillars

detailed_explanation.summary:
HFpEF Management (AHA/ACC/HFSA 2022 + ESC 2023 Update):

(1) Diagnosis: clinical HF + EF ≥ 50% + structural/functional evidence diastolic dysfunction
detailed_explanation.reason: HFpEF: confirm with H2FPEF/HFA-PEFF + workup etiology. SGLT2i Class I (EMPEROR-Preserved, DELIVER). MRA + ARNI selected. Diuretics for congestion. Treat comorbidities (HTN, AFib, obesity, CKD, OSA, iron). Amyloid workup. Multidisciplinary.

reference_source: AHA Get With Guidelines HF; AHA Transitions

detailed_explanation.summary:
Post-HF Hospitalization Transitions of Care (AHA + ACC + STS):

(1) Vulnerable period: 30 days post-discharge — high readmission (~ 25%) + mortality
detailed_explanation.reason: Post-HF discharge: 30-day vulnerable period. Early visit 7-14 d. Medication reconciliation + GDMT titration. Volume monitoring + education. Cardiac rehab. Comorbidity + depression. Multidisciplinary care + remote monitoring. Reduces readmission.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 68 ปี HTN + DM — new AFib detected on routine ECG, HR 88 (rate-controlled), asymptomatic; CHA2DS2-VASc 4

detailed_explanation.summary:
New AFib Outpatient Management (ACC/AHA/HRS 2023):

(1) Workup: history + ECG + TSH + electrolytes + CBC + Cr + echo (LVEF, valvular, LA size, structural)
detailed_explanation.reason: New AFib: workup + reversible causes. CHA2DS2-VASc → DOAC for stroke prevention (preferred over warfarin). Rate first-line most; early rhythm control (EAST-AFNET 4) for selected. Risk factor management (LEGACY). Multidisciplinary.

choices.text: Emergency PCI for all stable angina

detailed_explanation.summary:
Stable Angina (CCS) Outpatient (AHA/ACC Chronic CAD 2023):

(1) Diagnostic workup: pretest probability (typical anginal, age, sex, risk factors)
detailed_explanation.reason: Stable angina: pretest probability + stress test or CCTA. OMT (ASA + statin + anti-anginal + ACEi/ARB/SGLT2 selected). Revascularization for refractory or high-risk anatomy (ISCHEMIA — OMT often equivalent). Risk factor modification + cardiac rehab. Multidisciplinary.

choices.label: A
choices.text: Send to ED for all exacerbations

detailed_explanation.summary:
COPD Exacerbation Outpatient Management (GOLD 2024):

(1) Severity assessment: vital signs, oximetry, mental status, accessory muscle use, ability to speak
detailed_explanation.reason:
COPD exacerbation outpatient: severity assessment. Bronchodilator intensify + prednisone 40 mg × 5 d (REDUCE). Abx if Anthonisen ≥

2. O2 target 88-92%. Close F/U + pulmonary rehab + triggers + adherence. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pediatric Asthma Management (GINA 2024 Pediatric + EPR-4):

(1) Severity classification: this = uncontrolled / moderate persistent — needs step up
detailed_explanation.reason: Pediatric asthma uncontrolled: step up to low-dose ICS-LABA + SABA PRN. Inhaler technique. Action plan + trigger avoidance. Comorbidity. Vaccinations. Never LABA monotherapy. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
CKD Stage 3 (G3b A2) Management — Primary Care (KDIGO 2022 + ADA 2024):

(1) Confirm + stage: 2 eGFR < 60 over 3 mo + albuminuria
detailed_explanation.reason: CKD stage 3: BP < 130/80 + ACEi/ARB + SGLT2i (DAPA-CKD, EMPA-KIDNEY) + finerenone (DM+CKD) + GLP-1. Glycemic. Statin. Avoid nephrotoxins. Screen complications. Refer nephrology < 30 or rapid decline. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
CKD Anemia Management (KDIGO Anemia 2012 + 2018 Update):

(1) Workup: CBC, retic, iron studies (ferritin, TSAT), B12, folate, TSH, occult blood, evaluate for blood loss
detailed_explanation.reason: CKD anemia: workup + iron first (IV preferred CKD 4-5). ESA when Hb < 10, target 10-11.5 (TREAT — avoid > 11.5). HIF-PHI emerging oral option. Transfusion only acute symptomatic. Multidisciplinary nephrology.

Pediatric Obesity Management (AAP 2023 + Endocrine Society):

detailed_explanation.reason: Pediatric obesity AAP 2023: no watchful waiting. IHBLT ≥ 26 h. Pharmacotherapy ≥ 12 yo (GLP-1 RA effective). Bariatric ≥ 13 yo selected. Treat comorbidities. Family-centered + non-stigmatizing. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acute Otitis Media Pediatric (AAP 2013 + 2022 Update):

(1) Diagnostic criteria: middle ear effusion + signs of inflammation (bulging TM, otorrhea not from external) + moderate-severe symptoms
detailed_explanation.reason: Pediatric AOM: criteria (effusion + inflammation + sx). Treat < 6 mo, severe, bilateral young. Observation option non-severe + reliable F/U. Amoxicillin high-dose first-line × 10 d; amox-clav if treatment failure/recent abx. Vaccines for prevention.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 40 ปี — nasal congestion + facial pain × 12 d, ไม่ดีขึ้น, purulent rhinorrhea เพิ่ม + fever กลับมา; no severe sx initially

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acute Sinusitis (IDSA + AAFP 2012 + ICAR 2021):

(1) Acute bacterial rhinosinusitis (ABRS) criteria — any 1 of:
  • Persistent symptoms ≥ 10 d without improvement
detailed_explanation.reason: ABRS criteria: persistent ≥ 10 d, severe ≥ 3-4 d at onset, or worsening (double-sickening). Amox-clav first-line. Saline irrigation + intranasal steroid. Reassess 48-72 h. Watch complications. Chronic sinusitis — ENT.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 8 ปี — bilateral red eye + watery discharge × 2 d, no pain, no vision change; URI symptoms; ไม่มี contact lens

detailed_explanation.summary:
Conjunctivitis Differential + Management (AAO + AAFP):

(1) Differentiate by features:
  • Viral (most common — adenovirus): watery discharge, often URI + preauricular LN, bilateral progression, highly contagious
detailed_explanation.reason: Conjunctivitis: differentiate viral (watery, URI, preauricular LN) vs bacterial (purulent) vs allergic (itchy) vs HSV/gonococcal (urgent). Red flags → ophthalmology. Viral — supportive. Bacterial — topical abx selective. Allergic — topical antihistamine.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acute Infectious Gastroenteritis (IDSA 2017 + WHO):

(1) Most acute diarrhea = viral self-limited (norovirus, rotavirus, adenovirus)
detailed_explanation.reason: Acute GE: mostly viral self-limited. ORT first. Resume diet ASAP. Loperamide for non-bloody adult OK; avoid invasive/EHEC. Antibiotic targeted not empiric. Workup for severe/prolonged/bloody/immunocompromised. C. diff considerations.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Chronic Insomnia Management (AASM 2017 + ACP):

(1) Diagnosis chronic insomnia disorder: difficulty initiating/maintaining sleep ≥ 3x/wk × ≥ 3 mo + daytime impairment + not better explained by other
detailed_explanation.reason: Chronic insomnia: CBT-I first-line (ACP). Sleep hygiene. Pharmacotherapy short-term — DORAs/ramelteon/doxepin preferred; avoid benzo + diphenhydramine (Beers). Address comorbidity. Digital CBT-I accessible. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Panic Disorder Management (APA + AAFP):

(1) Diagnosis DSM-5: recurrent unexpected panic attacks + ≥ 1 mo of persistent worry about attacks or maladaptive behavior change + not attributable to other
detailed_explanation.reason: Panic disorder: SSRI/SNRI + CBT first-line. Avoid long-term benzo (bridge only). Lifestyle (caffeine, exercise). Education + breathing. Comorbidity. Digital CBT + Collaborative Care. Effective treatment widely available.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Adult ADHD Diagnosis + Treatment (DIVA + ACEM + APA):

(1) DSM-5 criteria adult: ≥ 5 of 9 inattention or hyperactivity-impulsivity symptoms persistent ≥ 6 mo + onset before age 12 + multiple settings + functional impairment + not better explained by other
detailed_explanation.reason: Adult ADHD: DSM-5 + structured interview + collateral. Stimulants first-line (long-acting preferred); non-stimulants (atomoxetine, viloxazine, alpha-2) alternatives. CBT + accommodations. Comorbidity. CV evaluation. Underdiagnosed adult.

detailed_explanation.reason: AUD: USPSTF screening (AUDIT-C). SBIRT brief intervention. Naltrexone first-line pharmacotherapy (underused). Acamprosate, disulfiram options. Behavioral therapy combo best. Withdrawal management. Comorbidity + complications. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Opioid Use Disorder (OUD) Treatment (SAMHSA + ASAM):

(1) Diagnosis DSM-5: ≥ 2 of 11 criteria over 12 mo
detailed_explanation.reason: OUD: MOUD = buprenorphine (X-waiver removed 2023 — major access expansion), methadone (OTP), naltrexone — life-saving. Naloxone universal harm reduction. Behavioral adjunct. Pregnancy MOUD preferred. Address comorbidity + stigma. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Postpartum Depression (PPD) (ACOG + AAP + USPSTF):

(1) Universal screening — pregnancy + postpartum + well-child visits (mother) — USPSTF recommendation
detailed_explanation.reason: PPD: universal screening (EPDS). Assess safety + rule out psychosis. Mild-mod — psychotherapy; mod-severe — SSRI (sertraline first-line lactation) + therapy; brexanolone/zuranolone severe new options. SDOH + lactation + father screening. Multidisciplinary.

Postpartum Contraception (CDC US MEC + ACOG):

(1) Counseling: discuss prenatal + postpartum + at first postpartum visit
detailed_explanation.reason: Postpartum contraception: LARC preferred. CDC US MEC. Immediate postpartum IUD OK. 0-21 d avoid combined hormonal (VTE); progestin OK. HTN history → avoid combined; LARC, POPs OK. Counseling prenatal + postpartum.

Developmental Delay + Autism Screening (AAP + AAFP):

detailed_explanation.summary:
Pediatric ADHD Diagnosis + Treatment (AAP 2019 + AACAP):

(1) DSM-5 criteria pediatric (< 17 yo): ≥ 6 of 9 inattention or hyperactivity-impulsivity symptoms × ≥ 6 mo + onset < age 12 + ≥ 2 settings + functional impairment
detailed_explanation.reason: Pediatric ADHD: DSM-5 + multi-setting scales (Vanderbilt, Conners). 4-5 yo behavioral first; 6+ yo medication + behavioral + school. Stimulants first-line (ER preferred). Monitor growth, BP, mood. School accommodations. Family training. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Hyperemesis Gravidarum vs Common NV in Pregnancy (ACOG):

(1) NV of pregnancy (NVP): ~ 70-80% pregnancies, peak 6-12 wk, usually self-limited
detailed_explanation.reason: Pregnancy NV: stepwise. B6 + doxylamine first-line. Antihistamine + promethazine/metoclopramide. Ondansetron after others. IV hydration + thiamine if dehydrated. HG (> 5% weight loss) admit if needed. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Breastfeeding Support + Common Issues (USBC + AAP + ABM):

(1) Universal counseling pregnancy + postpartum: exclusive breastfeeding 6 mo + continued through 1-2 yr + complementary foods (WHO + AAP)
detailed_explanation.reason: Lactation: latch + positioning most common cause of pain/low supply. IBCLC early. Mastitis: continue feeding + abx if needed. Workplace support. Most meds safe (LactMed). Avoid early supplementation. Address PPD. Multidisciplinary.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Dementia Workup Primary Care (AAN + Alzheimer's Association)

choices.label: D
choices.text: Single visit referral to nursing home

detailed_explanation.summary:
Dementia Workup Primary Care (AAN + Alzheimer's Association):

(1) Cognitive assessment:
  • Brief screening: Mini-Cog (3-word recall + clock draw, ~ 3 min), MoCA (Montreal Cognitive Assessment, 30-pt, more sensitive than MMSE for MCI), MMSE
detailed_explanation.reason: Dementia workup: cognitive (Mini-Cog/MoCA) + functional (ADL/IADL) + collateral. Rule out reversible (TSH, B12, MRI, depression, meds). Subtype matters. Treatment: ChEI/memantine; new anti-amyloid early AD. Non-pharm + caregiver support + advance care planning. Multidisciplinary.

(1) CAM (Confusion Assessment Method) for diagnosis:
  • 1
detailed_explanation.reason: Delirium: CAM diagnosis. Hyperactive vs hypoactive (often missed). Multifactorial — identify causes (UTI, drugs, electrolyte, sensory). Non-pharm FIRST (HELP, ABCDEF, reorient, mobility, sleep). Antipsychotic LAST resort. Avoid benzo (except withdrawal). Prognosis serious. Multidisciplinary.

  • HIV: + 4th gen Ag/Ab

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acne Vulgaris Stepwise Management (AAD 2024):

(1) Severity assessment: mild (comedonal, few inflammatory), moderate (extensive comedonal + inflammatory papulopustules), severe (nodulocystic, scarring)
detailed_explanation.reason: Acne: stepwise. Mild — topical retinoid ± BP ± topical abx (combined w/ BP). Moderate — combination + oral doxycycline + OCP/spironolactone (women). Severe — isotretinoin (dermatology + iPLEDGE). Avoid abx monotherapy. Psychosocial + adjuncts. Multidisciplinary.

Suspicious Skin Lesion + Melanoma Approach (AAD + NCCN):

(1) ABCDE criteria for melanoma:
  • Asymmetry
detailed_explanation.reason: Suspicious pigmented lesion: ABCDE + risk factors → biopsy. Excisional with narrow margin preferred (not shave). Breslow depth key. Staging + treatment if melanoma (WLE + SLNB + adjuvant). Sun protection + screening. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Rotator Cuff Tendinopathy / Subacromial Impingement (AAOS + Cochrane):

(1) Diagnosis clinical: pain with overhead activity, lateral arm pain, night pain
detailed_explanation.reason: Shoulder impingement / RC tendinopathy: clinical diagnosis. Conservative first (4-6 wk) — PT gold standard + NSAIDs + activity mod. Subacromial steroid if persistent. Surgery for refractory or specific tears. Workplace + risk factors. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี runner — heel pain (medial calcaneal) worst with first steps morning + after rest × 3 mo

detailed_explanation.summary:
Plantar Fasciitis (APTA + AAOS):

(1) Clinical diagnosis: heel pain medial calcaneal, worst first steps morning + after rest (post-static dyskinesia), insertion of plantar fascia
detailed_explanation.reason: Plantar fasciitis: clinical diagnosis. Conservative first (90%+ resolve): stretching + footwear + activity mod + NSAIDs + PT. Refractory — steroid injection (limit), ESWT, PRP. Surgery rare. Weight, training, ergonomics. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 65 ปี BMI 33 — bilateral knee pain + crepitus + morning stiffness < 30 min × 1 yr, X-ray joint space narrowing

choices.label: B
choices.text: Knee OA Management Primary Care (AAOS 2021 + ACR/AF 2019 + OARSI)

detailed_explanation.summary:
Knee OA Management Primary Care (AAOS 2021 + ACR/AF 2019 + OARSI):

(1) Diagnosis: clinical + X-ray (joint space narrowing, osteophytes, subchondral sclerosis)
detailed_explanation.reason: Knee OA: weight loss + exercise FOUNDATION. Topical NSAIDs first-line; oral NSAIDs caution; duloxetine + capsaicin; AVOID opioids. Intra-articular steroid short-term. TKA for refractory + impairment. Arthroscopy for OA NOT recommended. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Travel Medicine Pre-Travel Visit (CDC Yellow Book + ISTM):

(1) Pre-travel assessment ideally 4-6 wk prior: itinerary (countries, regions, urban/rural, duration, season, accommodations, activities), traveler factors (age, comorbidity, pregnancy, immunosuppression, medications, prior travel, vaccines)
detailed_explanation.reason: Travel medicine: pre-travel 4-6 wk. Routine + required (yellow fever, meningococcal) + recommended (typhoid, HepA/B, rabies, JE) vaccines. Malaria chemoprophylaxis + bite avoidance. Traveler's diarrhea self-Rx + food safety. Other risks. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.reason: Graves: TSH ↓ + T4/T3 ↑ + TRAb +. 3 options: ATD (MMI; PTU 1st trim) / RAI / surgery — based on factors. Beta-blocker for sx. Orbitopathy — smoking cessation + steroid + teprotumumab. Pregnancy considerations. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
IBS Management (ACG 2021 + Rome IV):

(1) Diagnosis Rome IV: recurrent abdominal pain ≥ 1 d/wk in past 3 mo + ≥ 2 of: related to defecation, change in frequency, change in form
detailed_explanation.reason: IBS: Rome IV diagnosis + limited workup (no alarms). Education + dietary (low FODMAP, fiber). Subtype-specific pharm (IBS-D — rifaximin/loperamide/TCA; IBS-C — PEG/linaclotide). Behavioral therapy strong evidence. Address psychosocial. Multidisciplinary.

Cellulitis Outpatient Management (IDSA SSTI 2014):

(1) Diagnosis clinical: erythema + warmth + edema + tenderness
detailed_explanation.reason: Non-purulent cellulitis: clinical diagnosis, mark borders. Cephalexin (strep ± MSSA) 5-10 d outpatient if mild. Cover MRSA only if purulent / risk factors. Address entry (tinea), elevation. Watch necrotizing fasciitis. Follow-up 24-72 h.

Acute Gout + Long-term Management (ACR 2020):

(1) Acute attack diagnosis: classic (1st MTP — podagra), monoarthritis, rapid onset, red/hot/tender
detailed_explanation.reason:
Gout: acute — NSAID/colchicine/steroid (start < 24 h). Joint aspiration if uncertain. Long-term ULT (allopurinol — HLA-B*5801 high-risk pop; febuxostat alt) — start during attack OK with anti-inflam bridge. Target uric acid <

6. Lifestyle + CV risk + meds.

Rheumatoid Arthritis Initial Management (ACR 2021):

detailed_explanation.summary:
Erectile Dysfunction (ED) Evaluation + Management (AUA + ISSM):

(1) ED as window to systemic disease — strong marker of CV disease, often precedes by 3-5 yr
detailed_explanation.reason: ED: marker of CVD — comprehensive risk eval. PDE5 inhibitors first-line (avoid w/ nitrates). Address meds + low T (only if confirmed). Lifestyle + CV risk. Second-line: alprostadil, vacuum, prosthesis. Psychosexual counseling. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.reason: Menopause MHT: NAMS 2022 supports for symptomatic < 60 yo or < 10 yr from menopause. Transdermal preferred (lower VTE). Estrogen alone if no uterus; +progestin if uterus. Local vaginal for GSM. Non-hormonal options (SSRI, gabapentin, fezolinetant). Individualized + shared decision. Multidisciplinary.

Combined OCP for menses + hyperandrogenism + contraception; spironolactone add-on; metformin for metabolic. Letrozole for ovulation induction. Endometrial protection. Screen metabolic + mental health. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Infant Fever Workup (AAP 2021 Guideline: Well-Appearing Febrile Infants 8-60 d):

(1) All febrile infants < 28 d (this patient — 5 wk = 35 d at edge):
  • Full sepsis workup
detailed_explanation.reason: Infant fever 29-60 d (AAP 2021): risk-stratify with inflammatory markers. Required workup (CBC, procal, UA, cx, blood cx). LP if high-risk. Empiric ceftriaxone if treating. Pathogens: GBS, E coli, Listeria, HSV. < 28 d: full workup + admit always. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Bronchiolitis Management (AAP 2014 + Continuing Updates):

(1) Diagnosis clinical: < 2 yo + viral URI + wheeze/crackles + work of breathing
detailed_explanation.reason: Bronchiolitis: clinical, no routine testing, supportive (hydration, O2 if < 90, suction). Avoid: albuterol, steroid, abx routinely. Admit if severe. Prevention: nirsevimab all infants + maternal RSV vaccine (2023 update). Family education + follow-up.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acute Otitis Externa (AAO-HNS 2014):

(1) Diagnosis: rapid onset + canal inflammation + ear pain ± otorrhea ± hearing loss
detailed_explanation.reason: Acute OE: clinical. Pseudomonas + Staph. Topical antibiotic ± steroid (FQ drops safe perforation). Pain control. Avoid water. Oral abx only severe/extension/DM. Malignant OE — emergency. Prevention. Multidisciplinary.

(1) Universal screening: Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale (C-SSRS) or ASQ (Ask Suicide-Screening Questions) for all behavioral health + medical patients (Joint Commission requirement)
detailed_explanation.reason: Post-suicide attempt: comprehensive risk assessment (C-SSRS), risk + protective factors. Inpatient vs intensive outpatient. Safety planning + means restriction (firearms #1). Treat underlying. Lithium/clozapine anti-suicidal. Caring contacts. Multidisciplinary.

Eating Disorder (Anorexia Nervosa) — Primary Care (AAP + APA):

(1) Diagnosis DSM-5 AN: restriction of intake → significantly low weight, intense fear of gaining weight, body image disturbance
detailed_explanation.reason: Anorexia: high mortality. Comprehensive medical eval + hospitalization criteria. Multidisciplinary care mandatory. Adolescent — FBT first-line. CBT-E adult. Pharm limited AN (olanzapine some). Refeeding syndrome prevention. Bone health. Specialty programs.

Giant Cell Arteritis (GCA / Temporal Arteritis) + PMR — EMERGENCY (ACR 2022 + EULAR):

(1) GCA medical emergency: risk of permanent blindness (ischemic optic neuropathy)
detailed_explanation.reason: GCA = emergency (blindness risk) — start high-dose prednisone IMMEDIATELY on suspicion; biopsy after. Tocilizumab steroid-sparing. PMR — prednisone 15-20 mg, slow taper. Aortitis surveillance. Steroid management + bone. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Iron Deficiency Anemia (IDA) Workup + Management:

(1) Diagnosis: microcytic anemia (low MCV) + low ferritin (gold standard if < 30
detailed_explanation.reason: IDA: low ferritin + low TSAT + microcytic. Always find cause: menstrual, GI (endoscopy if older/male/no source), malabsorption (celiac), diet. Oral iron first-line (alternate-day improves absorption). IV iron if oral fails. Treat cause. Recheck Hb 2-4 wk.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Familial Hypercholesterolemia (FH) (NLA + AHA):

(1) High suspicion: severe LDL (> 190 untreated adult, > 160 child), family history premature CAD, physical findings (tendinous xanthomas, arcus cornealis < 45 yo, xanthelasma)
detailed_explanation.reason: FH: high suspicion (severe LDL + family history + xanthoma). Treat very high-risk. Goals LDL < 100 (or < 70 if ASCVD). Aggressive: high-intensity statin + ezetimibe + PCSK9i / inclisiran. Cascade family screening + pediatric. Multidisciplinary.

(1) Pharmacogenomics (PGx): tailoring drug + dose based on genetic variants affecting metabolism, transport, target, or immune response
detailed_explanation.reason: Pharmacogenomics: CPIC + PharmGKB. Key in primary care: CYP2C19 + clopidogrel/PPI/SSRI; CYP2D6 + codeine; CYP2C9 + warfarin; HLA-B*1502/5801 (Asian) + CBZ/allopurinol; TPMT thiopurines; G6PD; SLCO1B1 statins. EHR integration. Multidisciplinary with pharmacy.

(1) High-risk screening: STOP-BANG ≥ 3 = increased risk (≥ 5 high), Epworth Sleepiness Scale > 10 = excessive daytime sleepiness
detailed_explanation.reason: OSA: STOP-BANG screen, HSAT or PSG diagnosis. CPAP first-line (adherence challenge — support). Oral appliance, positional, weight loss alternatives/adjuncts. detailed_explanation.reason: LTBI: TST or IGRA + rule out active TB. Short-course preferred — 3HP (weekly INH-rifapentine) or 4R (rifampin daily). Baseline LFT + monitoring. Drug interactions (rifampin). Adherence support. HIV testing. Contact investigation. Multidisciplinary.

TIA / Minor Stroke Management (AHA/ASA 2021):

(1) TIA definition (modern): transient neuro deficit without infarction on imaging
detailed_explanation.reason: TIA = high stroke risk — urgent ED workup. MRI DWI + CTA + echo + AFib monitoring. Acute: aspirin + statin + DAPT for high-risk × 21-90 d. Etiology-specific (CEA, anticoag for AFib). Risk factor modification. Multidisciplinary.

BPPV (Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo) (AAO-HNS 2017):

(1) Most common cause peripheral vertigo
detailed_explanation.reason: BPPV: brief positional vertigo. Dix-Hallpike diagnosis posterior canal. Epley maneuver first-line — highly effective. Differentiate central (HiNTS). Vestibular suppressants limited role. Vestibular rehab for persistent. Multidisciplinary.

(1) Classification ARIA: intermittent vs persistent
detailed_explanation.reason: Allergic rhinitis: ARIA classification. INCS first-line (correct technique). Oral 2nd-gen antihistamine adjunct. Saline irrigation. Avoid first-gen antihistamines + chronic decongestants. Allergen immunotherapy for refractory + modifies disease. Treat comorbidities. Multidisciplinary.

choices.text: Same-day return to play if asymptomatic

detailed_explanation.summary:
Sport-Related Concussion (Berlin/Amsterdam Consensus + AAN):

(1) Definition: traumatic brain injury induced by biomechanical forces, with rapid onset of neurological dysfunction
detailed_explanation.reason: Concussion: SCAT assessment + rule out cervical/ICH. Brief rest then early active rehab (NOT prolonged rest). Graduated return to play + learn. Symptom-specific treatment. Multidisciplinary. NO same-day RTP. Watch persistent + repeat. Multidisciplinary.

choices.label: E
choices.text: IV vitamin D for mild deficiency

detailed_explanation.summary:
Vitamin D Deficiency Management (Endocrine Society 2024 + IOM):

(1) Definitions (controversy):
  • Endocrine Society: deficiency < 20 ng/mL (50 nmol/L), insufficiency 20-29
detailed_explanation.reason: Vitamin D deficiency: targeted screening (Endocrine 2024 less aggressive). D3 preferred. Loading 50,000 IU weekly × 8 wk; maintenance 1,000-2,000 IU daily. Recheck. Address other symptom causes. Lifestyle + sun. VITAL — no CV/cancer benefit in sufficient. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Emergency Contraception (ACOG + WHO 2024):

(1) Options effectiveness (most to least):
  • Copper IUD (Cu-IUD) — most effective EC (~ 99%)
detailed_explanation.reason: EC: most effective Cu-IUD (or LNG-IUD) within 5 d. UPA up to 120 hr (better BMI > 25). LNG up to 72 hr (OTC). Mechanism — delay ovulation. Address ongoing contraception. STI screening. Sexual assault considerations. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 40 ปี — incidental HBsAg positive; ALT normal; HBeAg neg, anti-HBe pos; HBV DNA 8000 IU/mL; clinically asymptomatic

detailed_explanation.summary:
Chronic Hepatitis B Management (AASLD 2018 + 2022 updates):

(1) Phases CHB:
  • Immune tolerant (HBeAg+, high VL, normal ALT, minimal liver disease — usually young)

(1) Palliative care = serious illness care — at any stage, alongside curative treatment

detailed_explanation.reason: Thyroid nodule: TSH + US for all. TI-RADS or ATA pattern guides FNA threshold (size + features). Bethesda categorization → management. Molecular testing for indeterminate. Surgical pathology directed treatment. Active surveillance selected. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Acute Uncomplicated Diverticulitis (AGA 2015 + 2022):

(1) Diagnosis: CT contrast preferred imaging (also assesses complications — abscess, perforation, fistula, obstruction)
detailed_explanation.reason: Acute uncomplicated diverticulitis: CT diagnosis. Outpatient OK selected — antibiotics selective (AGA 2015), often supportive sufficient. Colonoscopy 4-8 wk post for first. Long-term high-fiber + lifestyle. Surgery individualized. Watch complications. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 55 ปี — microscopic hematuria (5 RBC/HPF) ตอนทำ annual exam; no UTI; smoker × 30 yr; HT

detailed_explanation.summary:
Microscopic Hematuria Workup (AUA 2020):

(1) Diagnosis: ≥ 3 RBC/HPF on properly collected midstream urine confirmed on 1 of 2 urinalyses
detailed_explanation.reason: Microscopic hematuria: AUA 2020 risk-stratified workup. High-risk (smoker, > 60, > 25 RBC) → cystoscopy + CT urography. Rule out UTI/menstruation first. Glomerular features → nephrology. Smoking cessation. Don't dismiss bladder CA. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 45 ปี — radicular leg pain + numbness + L5 distribution × 4 wk; no red flags; conservative therapy failing

detailed_explanation.summary:
Lumbar Radiculopathy / Sciatica Management (ACP + NASS):

(1) Diagnosis: history (radiating leg pain > back pain, dermatomal, exacerbated by Valsalva, sciatic stretch — SLR + crossed SLR), exam (neurologic — sensory, motor, reflexes)
detailed_explanation.reason: Sciatica: rule out cauda equina. No imaging first 4-6 wk without red flags. Conservative (PT, NSAIDs, activity, education). MRI if red flags / refractory / progressive deficit. ESI selective. Surgery for emergent or refractory + correlative imaging. Yellow flags. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 35 ปี — acute unilateral facial weakness (forehead + face) × 1 d, decreased tear + taste, ปวดหลังหู; no other neuro deficit

detailed_explanation.summary:
Bell's Palsy Management (AAN 2012 + AAO-HNS 2013):

(1) Diagnosis clinical: acute (< 72 h) peripheral CN VII palsy involving forehead (unlike central — preserves forehead)
detailed_explanation.reason: Bell's palsy: peripheral CN VII (forehead involved). Rule out central, Lyme, Ramsay Hunt. Treatment within 72 h: prednisone 60 mg × 7 d → taper. Antiviral selected for severe. Eye care critical. Prognosis good — most recover. Refractory specialist. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 25 ปี — generalized hives + lip swelling + dyspnea + dizziness 15 min after eating shellfish; BP 90/60

detailed_explanation.summary:
Anaphylaxis — Emergency (AAAAI + WAO 2020):

(1) Diagnosis: rapid-onset (minutes to hours) of skin/mucosa involvement + ≥ 1 of (respiratory, hypotension/end-organ, GI, syncope)
detailed_explanation.reason: Anaphylaxis: IM epinephrine IMMEDIATELY (0.3-0.5 mg vastus lateralis), repeat q 5-15 min if needed. Adjuncts (O2, IV fluid, H1/H2 antihistamine, bronchodilator, steroid) AFTER epi. Observation biphasic. Discharge with epi pen + plan + allergist referral. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: พ่อแม่มาขอเลื่อนวัคซีน MMR ลูก 12 mo เพราะกังวลเรื่อง autism + กลัวการเจ็บปวดของลูก

detailed_explanation.summary:
Vaccine Hesitancy Counseling (CDC + AAP + AAFP):

(1) Common parental concerns:
  • Autism (myth from Wakefield retracted 1998 — extensively disproven across many large studies, > 1 million children)
detailed_explanation.reason: Vaccine hesitancy: presumptive recommendation; MI for hesitant; address concerns with evidence (MMR-autism DISPROVEN); CASE approach. Continue relationship + document. Resources. Real disease risks (measles re-emergence). State/school requirements. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี — bright red rectal bleeding (BRBPR) per stool × 2 wk + perianal discomfort, no weight loss, last colonoscopy 5 yr ago normal

detailed_explanation.summary:
Rectal Bleeding (BRBPR) Workup (AGA + ACG):

(1) History: characterize bleeding (with/separate from stool, amount, color), associated (pain, itching, discharge, mass, change in bowel habits, weight loss, family history CRC, IBD, smoking, NSAIDs)
detailed_explanation.reason: Rectal bleeding: characterize + DRE + anoscopy. Causes (hemorrhoid common, but rule out malignancy especially ≥ 45 yo). Colonoscopy threshold low (age ≥ 40-45 or red flags). Hemorrhoid + fissure conservative + procedures + surgery if refractory. STI proctitis screening. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.summary:
Comprehensive 6-Week Postpartum Visit (ACOG 2018 + AAFP):

(1) ACOG 2018 update — postpartum care as ongoing process not single visit — 'The 4th trimester' framework:
  • Initial contact (in-person or phone) within 3 wk
detailed_explanation.reason: Postpartum care: '4th trimester' ongoing not single visit (ACOG 2018). Comprehensive — mental health (PPD/anxiety), reproductive/contraception, lactation, physical recovery, chronic conditions (BP/DM as CV risk markers), pelvic floor PT. Father screening. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 70 ปี — Na 121, confused; chronic diuretic + SSRI; volume euvolemic by exam; urine Na 65, urine osm 380, serum osm 256

detailed_explanation.summary:
Hyponatremia Workup + Management Primary Care:

(1) Severity: mild (Na 130-134), moderate (125-129), severe (< 125)
detailed_explanation.reason: Hyponatremia: volume + serum/urine osm + urine Na for diagnosis. SIADH common (drugs — SSRI; CNS; lung; postop). Careful correction to avoid ODS — max 8-10 mmol/L/24 h. Treat underlying + offending drugs. Severe → hypertonic saline ICU. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.reason: Immunocompromised: types (primary + secondary). Workup if recurrent/unusual. Vaccines: avoid live, more doses, recombinant Shingrix preferred. choices.label: D

detailed_explanation.summary:
Outpatient Hyperkalemia Management:

(1) Severity: mild 5.5-5.9, moderate 6.0-6.4, severe ≥ 6.5 or any ECG changes or symptoms — affects urgency
detailed_explanation.reason: Hyperkalemia: severity + ECG + cause assessment. Pseudo? Mild-mod outpatient — dietary, loop diuretic, new K binders (patiromer, ZS-9) allow RAAS continuation. Severe → ED (Ca, insulin/glucose, beta-agonist, dialysis). Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี — bilateral hand numbness + paresthesia at night, thumb/index/middle finger + lateral ring, weakness gripping × 6 mo

detailed_explanation.summary:
Carpal Tunnel Syndrome (AAOS + AAN):

(1) Diagnosis clinical primarily:
  • Symptoms: median nerve distribution numbness/paresthesia (thumb, index, middle, lateral ring), often worse at night/with activities, hand shaking relief, weakness/clumsiness
detailed_explanation.reason: Carpal tunnel: clinical diagnosis (provocative tests). EMG/NCS if uncertain/surgical. Conservative first (splint, ergonomics, steroid injection, NSAIDs). Surgery for severe/refractory/motor weakness. Address underlying conditions. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 50 ปี — chronic ear fullness + hearing loss + occasional otorrhea ข้างขวา × 6 mo; perforation in TM on otoscopy + scant drainage

detailed_explanation.summary:
Chronic Otitis Media Management (AAO-HNS):

(1) Chronic suppurative otitis media (CSOM): chronic TM perforation + intermittent/persistent otorrhea ≥ 2 wk
detailed_explanation.reason: Chronic OM / cholesteatoma: chronic perforation + otorrhea or pearly mass. Workup: otoscopy + audiogram + CT. Topical FQ + steroid drops safe with perforation. Cholesteatoma needs surgery. Complications serious. ENT referral. Multidisciplinary.

detailed_explanation.reason: Croup: viral parainfluenza most common. Severity (Westley). Clinical diagnosis. Single-dose dexamethasone for all (mild oral, moderate-severe + neb epinephrine). detailed_explanation.summary:
Multi-Generational Family-Centered Chronic Disease (AAFP + family medicine principles):

(1) Family medicine unique value: care entire family unit
detailed_explanation.reason: Multi-generational family-centered: family medicine unique value. Genogram + family assessment. Coordinated visits + communication. Caregiver support critical. Pediatric / adult / geriatric chronic disease in family context. Family-based interventions evidence. Cultural diversity. Adverse experiences. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: Hospital + employer — workplace wellness + occupational health primary care

choices.label: B
choices.text: Workplace Wellness + Occupational Health Integrative (NIOSH + ACOEM + WHO)

detailed_explanation.summary:
Workplace Wellness + Occupational Health Integrative (NIOSH + ACOEM + WHO):

(1) Workplace impact on health:
  • Working-age adults spend 1/3 of waking hours at work
detailed_explanation.reason: Workplace wellness + occupational health: huge impact on health. Exposures (physical, chemical, biological, psychosocial, shift work). Occupational history. Specific conditions. Workplace wellness programs (mixed evidence + equity). Return to work. Workers' comp. Vulnerable workers. Pandemic + climate. Multidisciplinary.

scenario: ผู้ป่วยอายุ 60 ปี — recent death of spouse × 2 mo + sleep + appetite + sadness + isolation; family worried

detailed_explanation.summary:
Bereavement + Grief — Primary Care (NHPCO + APA):

(1) Grief is normal universal response to loss, not pathology
detailed_explanation.reason: Bereavement / grief: normal universal response. Types + manifestations. Distinguish normal vs prolonged grief disorder (DSM-5-TR 2022). Comorbid depression + PTSD + suicide risk. PC role: presence, validation, education, screening, judicious meds (SSRI for comorbid), referrals. Resources + support groups. Special populations. Cultural humility. Provider self-care. Multidisciplinary.

Future of Primary Care + Family Medicine (NAM Implementing High-Quality Primary Care 2021 + AAFP):
